items.headline: City launches business license amnesty program for local shops
items.summary: Cupertino is offering an amnesty program to help unlicensed businesses in shopping centers get compliant with city regulations. The city is also rolling out new incentive programs for retail, small businesses, and startups to boost the local economy.

items.headline: Mixed-use housing development approved for East Middlefield Road
items.summary: Mountain View City Council approved a development agreement for a mixed-use residential project at 490 East Middlefield Road on the second reading at the March 24 meeting. This project will add new housing to the area.

items.headline: Mountain View applies for state housing incentive funding
items.summary: The city submitted an application for Round 4 of California's Prohousing Incentive Program at the March 24 meeting. If awarded, this funding could support local housing development initiatives.

items.headline: City seeking public input on federal housing grant spending plan
items.summary: Santa Clara is accepting public comments on how to spend HUD federal housing funds for the 2026-27 fiscal year. Residents who care about affordable housing or housing programs should review the draft plan and consider providing feedback.

items.headline: City holding community meeting on safe parking program
items.summary: Sunnyvale is hosting a public meeting on Monday, March 23 at 5:30 p.m. to discuss a safe parking initiative. This program likely addresses vehicle dwelling and homelessness in the city, and residents can learn details and provide input.
